title: fraxinus americana subsp. oregona
intro: Fraxinus americana subsp. oregona, also known as Oregon Ash or Oregon White Ash, is a subspecies of the White Ash tree, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana subsp. oregona include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and potential interactions with other medications.
